Intra-study association (focused replication across models)

Source: R/assoc_intra_study_association_replication.R
assoc_intra_study_association_replication.Rd

Re-run association analysis focusing on genomic regions that are statistically significant in a reference study, then merge results across two datasets for comparative inference.

Usage

assoc_intra_study_association_replication(
  inference_details_origin,
  inference_details,
  result_folder,
  maxResources = 90,
  parallel_strategy = "multicore",
  start_fresh = FALSE,
  ...
)

Arguments

inference_details_origin

data.frame. Inference parameters for the reference (origin) study whose significant regions define the search space.

inference_details

data.frame. Inference parameters for the target study on which the focused association analysis is performed.

result_folder

character. Path to the SEMseeker result folder.

maxResources

numeric. Maximum percentage of CPU cores to use (default 90).

parallel_strategy

character. Parallelisation backend passed to future; e.g. "multicore", "multisession", "sequential" (default "multicore").

start_fresh

logical. If TRUE, delete previous results before running (default FALSE).

...

Additional named arguments passed to core_init_env().

Value

Invisibly NULL. Results are written to the inference sub-folder of result_folder.

Examples

result_dir <- tempdir()
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
assoc_intra_study_association_replication(
  inference_details_origin = inference_study1,
  inference_details        = inference_study2,
  result_folder            = "~/semseeker_comparison/"
)
} # }
